channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NH Collection Resort to Boost Wellness and Luxury Tourism in Pemba Island
item.description: Pemba Island in Zanzibar is set to see a big step in luxury tourism with the upcoming NH Collection Pemba Wellness Resort, a high-end development worth over $70 million by Infinity Developments. The r...

item.title: Armoured Mobility becomes authorised armouring partner for Volkswagen South Africa
item.description: Armoured Mobility has officially been recognised as an approved armouring partner for Volkswagen South Africa. The announcement was made during the 2026 NAMPO Harvest Day in Bothaville, Free State. Th...

item.title: Challenges and Conditions in Approving Alberta's West Coast Pipeline
item.description: The proposed West Coast pipeline by Alberta, discussed in a memorandum of understanding with the federal government, faces multiple political and regulatory hurdles. Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has...

item.title: Tanzania Seeks Russian Investment Ahead of Afcon 2027 and Economic Growth
item.description: Tanzania is calling on Russian investors to work together with the local private sector to take advantage of opportunities arising from preparations for the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon), which T...

item.title: An MBA Graduate Navigates Challenges in the Corporate World
item.description: This excerpt from Mamta Kalia's novel 'Rat Race', translated by Jerry Pinto, follows Pawan Pande, a freshly graduated MBA who begins his career in Ahmedabad with the Noorani Oil company. Pawan enters ...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard monitors repeated presence of Chinese vessels in the EEZ
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has reported renewed sightings of Chinese militia and research vessels operating within the country’s Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) in the West Philippine Sea. Rear Ad...

item.title: Trump’s IRS lawsuit settlement could fund January 6 defendants
item.description: President Donald Trump's previous promise to donate any proceeds from his $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS to charity now appears to be reversed. National security journalist Marcy Wheeler reports ...

item.title: Political parties face scrutiny over nomination race controversies and vetting issues
item.description: The article discusses ongoing controversies within Canadian political party nomination processes, focusing on recent events in the Ontario Liberal Party. Nate Erskine-Smith, a departing federal Libera...

item.title: Rafael Jodar Emerges as Spain’s Rising Tennis Star at 19
item.description: Spanish teenager Rafael Jodar, often dubbed 'Rafa 2.0', is making waves on the professional tennis circuit. At just 19 years old, Jodar has transitioned from college tennis at the University of Virgin...

item.title: UAE commits $5 billion investment in India during PM Modi's Abu Dhabi visit
item.description: During Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s official visit to Abu Dhabi, the United Arab Emirates announced an investment commitment of around $5 billion in India, further strengthening the growing economic...

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway's First‑Quarter Portfolio Moves Under Greg Abel
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway revealed its first‑quarter portfolio changes as Greg Abel begins his tenure as CEO following Warren Buffett’s succession. The conglomerate disclosed that it built a sizable new posi...

item.title: South Africa Maintains Coal Exports to Israel Despite Activist Pressure
item.description: Trade Minister Parks Tau has confirmed in Parliament that South Africa has no plans to review its coal exports to Israel. In 2025, South Africa exported 1.8-million tons of coal to Israel, representin...

item.title: Uganda Confirms Imported Case of Ebola from DR Congo
item.description: The Uganda Health Ministry don confirm say Ebola don show for the country after one man die wey come from Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC). The deceased na 59-year-old man wey get Ebola Bundibugyo s...

item.title: Janet Delaney Explores Her Father’s Life as a Beauty Industry Salesman Through Photography
item.description: In her book 'Too Many Products Too Much Pressure,' Bay Area artist Janet Delaney revisits one of her earliest photography projects, documenting the final days of her father’s career as a beauty indust...

item.title: Access to full news article unavailable due to website restrictions
item.description: The content of the news article could not be accessed. The page provided was a blockage notice from News Corp Australia, indicating that the user or system accessing the site was detected as a crawler...

item.title: Knox County Schools in Tennessee restrict 'Roots' over single chapter deemed age-inappropriate
item.description: Knox County Schools in Tennessee have banned Alex Haley's 1976 Pulitzer-winning novel 'Roots: The Saga of an American Family' due to a passage in chapter 84 that the district deemed age-inappropriate ...

item.title: Global bond yields rise, slowing S&P 500 momentum amid market concerns
item.description: Global financial markets retreated as investors reacted to rising bond yields and concerns over persistent inflation. US stocks followed a downward trend set by European and Asian markets, with the S&...

item.title: Foundation stones laid for AMCA project, Drone City, and defence facilities in Andhra Pradesh
item.description: In a major advancement for India's defence sector, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh and Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu laid the foundation stone for several key defence projects in t...

item.title: Growth in South Africa's HIV Treatment Programme Slows in 2025
item.description: South Africa's antiretroviral (ARV) treatment programme saw very limited growth from 2024 to 2025, increasing by only 40 000 people to reach 6.18 million, according to the latest Thembisa model estima...

item.title: Federal Court rules transgender women cannot be excluded from women-only spaces
item.description: In a landmark ruling, the Federal Court of Australia has confirmed that transgender women are protected under gender discrimination laws. The case, Giggle for Girls v Tickle, arose when Roxanne Tickle...
